person.name: Margaret Grey
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary: Maud Grey, d/o Thomas Grey of Heton & Joan Mowbray [some antiquarian pedigrees incorrectly show her mother as Alice Neville]; was married to Robert Ogle, Knt. [of Ogle, Hepple, Saltwick, & Shilvington, co. Northumb and Nedirton, Durham], on about 21 May 1399. Hist. of Parl. confirms that she had three (3) sons and four (4) daughters, but Ogle (1902) included three more daughters. Hist. of Parl states that Maud's four (4) confirmed daughters were married: "to the sons of, respectively, Robert Harbottle, John Manners, Sir John Middleton and William Mitford."

sourceList.title: Matilda Grey Ogle, "Find A Grave Index"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Grey於大約1382年出生在英國諾圖波藍德可恩爾 奧 特維德時，她的父親Sir Thomas Gray Knt.25歲，母親Joan Mowbray19歲。她於大約1399年5月21日與Robert Ogle結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1451年8月21日之後在英國英格蘭。

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Grey was born about 1382, in Cornhill on Tweed, Northumberland, England, her father, Sir Thomas Gray Knt., was 25 and her mother, Joan Mowbray, was 19. She married Robert Ogle about 21 May 1399. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. She died after 21 August 1451, in England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé Robert Ogle environ 21 mai 1399. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée après 21 août 1451, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Grey(이)가 대략 1382년 영국 노르스벌랜드 코른힐  트위드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Thomas Gray Knt.(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Mowbray(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 대략 1399년 5월 21일 Robert Ogle(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1451년 8월 21일 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.
